mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates robust consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS 1-month change is positively correlated with 20-day SPX forward returns but Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated CPIAUCSL inflation alongside an accelerating FEDFUNDS rate creates a negative wealth effect for equities, as tightening financial conditions in a high-inflation environment compress risk premiums and valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS changes fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient at the 5% level in predicting 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO signals capacity utilization normalization and operating leverage recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this mechanism weakens once capacity is fully utilized.
failure_condition: In months classified as post-shock recovery, INDPRO acceleration does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher nominal FEDFUNDS levels increase the risk-free rate component of equity discount rates, mechanically reducing the present value of future corporate cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS level exhibits a Rank IC greater than -0.03 with 60-day SPX forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in HOUST of more than 100,000 units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and broader demand deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of months where HOUST declines by more than 100k units.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous deceleration of PAYEMS employment growth and contraction in RSAFS retail sales creates a demand-employment feedback loop that amplifies recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint negative changes in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not predict negative 20-day SPX returns with a hit rate above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 signals aggregate demand expansion that translates into higher expected corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly growth exhibits Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day SPX forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.
